full_prompt: Design a prestressed concrete beam for a collegiate precast-prestressed concrete beam design challenge. The beam shall be a single monolithic prestressed/precast concrete member with an overall length of 7.0 m; clear span 6.10 m (20 ft) center-to-center of supports with 450 mm overhangs on each end. Cross section: rectangular or I-section within a 305 mm (12 in) maximum depth and 305 mm maximum width bounding box. Concrete: normal-weight f'c = 55 MPa (8 ksi) at transfer day 1, 70 MPa (10 ksi) at 28 days; density 2400 kg/m^3; Young's modulus 4700 sqrt(f'c) = 39,340 MPa at 28 days. Prestressing: 7-wire low-relaxation strand 12.7 mm (0.5 in) diameter, ultimate strength 1860 MPa, modulus 196 GPa; initial prestress 0.75 f_pu = 1395 MPa. Mild steel: Grade 60 (fy = 414 MPa). Load cases: (R1) Third-point loading per ASTM C78 arrangement: applied factored live load >= 32 kip (142.3 kN) total (two-point loads of 16 kip each at the third-points) without structural failure. Safety: flexural capacity Mn shall provide a factored resistance phi Mn >= applied ultimate moment Mu under 1.2 DL + 1.6 x 32 kip LL; phi = 0.90. (R2) First cracking load >= 20 kip (89 kN) total (two 10 kip point loads at third points) - no visible cracking up to 20 kip service load. (R3) Midspan deflection at 20 kip service load <= span/360 = 6100 / 360 = 17 mm. (R4) Midspan deflection at 32 kip factored load <= span/180 = 34 mm AND the beam shall continue to take load past 32 kip with a ductility ratio (peak load / first-yield load) >= 1.5. (R5) Self-weight: beam mass per unit length to be computed and reported (scoring includes self-weight). Concrete material cost scored separately. (R6) Shear capacity phi Vn >= factored shear demand Vu under 1.6 x 32 kip loading with phi = 0.75; required shear capacity >= 142.3 kN. Primary verification: nonlinear structural FEA with concrete damaged plasticity or fiber-section beam model (for cracking and ductility), plus classical section analysis for pre-design. Vibration, thermal, fluid, radiation, buckling explicitly NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of beam geometry + rebar + strand layout, mix design, and a report listing beam mass per m, first cracking load in kN, ultimate flexural capacity in kN, midspan deflection at 20 kip and 32 kip in mm, ductility ratio.
short_prompt:
Design a precast prestressed concrete beam for a collegiate Big Beam-type challenge. The beam is a single monolithic member with 7.0 m overall length and 6.10 m (20 ft) clear span between supports (450 mm overhang at each end), tested in third-point loading per ASTM C78 arrangement. Concrete is normal-weight f'c = 55 MPa at day 1 (transfer) and 70 MPa at 28 days; prestress uses 12.7 mm 7-wire low-relaxation strand (f_pu 1860 MPa, modulus 196 GPa) tensioned to 0.75 f_pu = 1395 MPa; mild steel is Grade 60 (fy = 414 MPa). Load factors 1.2 DL + 1.6 LL for strength, phi = 0.90 flexure, phi = 0.75 shear.

Envelope: cross section rectangular or I-shaped within a 305 x 305 mm (12 x 12 in) bounding box.

Load cases.
- LC1 ultimate flexure: factored third-point loading of 32 kip (142.3 kN) total (16 kip per point); phi Mn must cover Mu under 1.2 DL + 1.6 x 32 kip LL.
- LC2 first cracking: 20 kip (89 kN) service load (10 kip per point) with no visible cracking.
- LC3 service deflection: midspan deflection at 20 kip <= span/360 = 17 mm; at 32 kip <= span/180 = 34 mm; post-32-kip ductility ratio (peak / first-yield) >= 1.5.
- LC4 shear: phi Vn >= factored 1.6 x 32 kip shear = 142.3 kN.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: R2 (cracking load) and R4 (ductility ratio) require nonlinear concrete + rebar material modeling (concrete damaged plasticity or fiber-section).

prompt.title: Prestressed precast concrete beam (collegiate design challenge)
prompt.brief: 7 m precast prestressed concrete beam, >= 32 kip factored capacity, no cracking to 20 kip, deflection limits at service and ultimate.

boundary_conditions.supports: pin at one end, roller at other on 6.1 m clear span with 450 mm overhangs

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: nonlinear concrete solid with embedded rebar (fiber section beam OK)
